title: Chapter 4: Equations, Inequalities and Graphs — Pyp

questions.question: Find the set of values of $x$ for which $|4x + 3| = 2x + 9$.
questions.explanation:
Set up two equations: $4x + 3 = 2x + 9 \implies$x$= 6 \implies x = 3$. Second case: $4x + 3 = -(2x + 9) \implies 4x + 3 = -2x - 9 \implies 6x = -12 \implies x = -2$. Testing both values in $2x + 9$ yields positive results ($15$ and 5), meaning both are fully valid solutions.

**Common mistake:** Students mistakenly reject $x = -2$ because it is a negative number, failing to notice that substituting it back into the right side expression $2x + 9$ still yields a valid positive value.

questions.question: Find the set of values of $x$ for which $2|x - 3| > |x + 6|$.
questions.explanation:
Square both sides: $4(x - 3)^2 > (x + 6)^2 \implies 4(x^2 - 6x + 9) > x^2 + 12x + 36 \implies 4x^2 - 24x + 36 > x^2 + 12x + 36$. Subtracting terms results in $3x^2 - 36x > 0 \implies 3x(x - 12) > 0$. The critical boundaries are^0 and 12. Since it is greater than zero, we choose the outer regions: $x < 0$ or $x > 12$.

**Common mistake:** Students often miss squaring the front constant coefficient^2, writing $2(x-3)^2$ instead of $4(x-3)^2$, which leads to completely incorrect algebraic boundaries.

questions.question: Find the set of values of $x$ satisfying the inequality $|5x - 2| \le 13$.
questions.explanation:
Rewrite as a continuous inequality: $-13 \le 5x - 2 \le 13$. Add^2 to all sections: -11 \le 5x \le 15

$$
. Divide by 5 to find the final solved set of values: -\frac{11}{5} \le x \le 3
$$.

**Common mistake:** Students commonly treat it as an outer interval inequality by misinterpreting the direction of the inequality sign, picking option B instead of the bounded inner set.
